subject: Pothole on Bohland finally fixed — thank you!
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to say thank you. That pothole in front of 2218 Bohland Ave has been tearing up my car every morning for two winters. I reported it in late March and honestly figured I'd be waiting until summer. But Public Works filled it within a week — and they did a clean job, not just a patch that falls apart in the rain. My neighbor noticed too. Small thing, I know, but it really does make a difference. Thank you to your crew.

subject: Taste of Bellwood was incredible this year
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo! I just got home with two very sticky, very happy kids and I had to email before I even put them to bed. The Taste of Bellwood was SO well organized this year — the layout made sense, the bathrooms were actually clean, and I loved the new kids' activity zone near the stage. Kevin and his team should be really proud. We've gone every year since 2021 and this was the best one by far. Already counting down to next summer.

subject: plow crew on rice ave
body_clean: hey mayor just wanted to say the plow guys came through rice ave at like 5:30am yesterday before the storm even stopped. whole block was clear before 7. my buddy on 25th ave said his street didn't get touched til noon lol but we were good. tell whoever drove that route they did great. appreciate it

subject: Water main repair on Harvard Ave — fast response
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I want to commend your Public Works department. We had a water main break at roughly 1407 Harvard Ave last Tuesday morning. I called the emergency line at 6 a.m. and by 9:30 a.m. a crew was on site. Service was restored before end of day. Given how disrupted our street was, I was bracing for a multi-day ordeal. Instead, the crew worked efficiently, cleaned up after themselves, and even repaved the small section of the roadway they had to cut. Excellent service.

subject: Water main repair on Harvard Ave — fast response
body_clean: Mr. Ferreira, thank you so much for taking the time to write. I'll be honest — hearing directly from residents when a crew does the job right is something I always pass along. Frank DiMeo and his team work hard under tough conditions, and this kind of feedback means a lot to them. I'm glad we got you back on line quickly. Please don't hesitate to reach out any time.

subject: Lorena in Water Billing is amazing
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I wanted to flag something positive for once. I had a billing dispute after our meter was apparently misread for two consecutive months — the bill was nearly double what it should have been. I called the water billing office and spoke with Lorena Diaz. She pulled my account, explained everything clearly, and had the credit processed within 48 hours. She was patient and professional even when I wasn't my most composed. That kind of service makes you feel like the Village actually cares about residents. Please pass along my thanks.

subject: Lorena in Water Billing is amazing
body_clean: Ms. Osei, thank you for writing — Mayor Okonkwo asked me to pass along his personal thanks for sharing this. He's forwarding your note to Lorena and her supervisor. It's genuinely wonderful to hear when a staff member goes the extra mile. We appreciate you taking a few minutes to let us know.

subject: Thank you — sidewalk on Marshall cleared quickly
body_clean: Mayor, I live on the 1600 block of Marshall Ave and there's a stretch of city-owned sidewalk near the old drainage ditch that nobody ever touched after snowfall. This year was different. Someone came through with a machine and cleared it within a day of the Feb 6th storm. I use a cane and that sidewalk is how I get to the pharmacy at 25th. I don't know who ordered it cleared, but it made a real difference for me and I suspect several other folks. Thank you.

subject: Thank you — sidewalk on Marshall cleared quickly
body_clean: Mr. Wachowski, thank you for this note. I'm genuinely glad the crew got to that stretch before you needed it. I added the Marshall Ave corridor to our priority sidewalk list at the start of the season precisely because of comments like yours from previous winters. Stay warm and safe, and please reach out if you ever run into anything that's not getting attention.

subject: Community garden plot program — wonderful idea
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. I just wrapped up my second season with one of the community garden plots and I really just wanted to say thank you for continuing the program. It sounds small but having that space on Eastern Ave has meant a great deal to me and my mother, who is 78 and doesn't get out much otherwise. She comes every Saturday. The staff that checks in — I believe it's the parks department — always makes sure the water spigots work and the paths are maintained. Please don't cut this program.

subject: Pothole crew on 25th Ave — great work
body_clean: Hi, I wanted to send a quick note of appreciation. The stretch of 25th Ave between Washington and St. Charles has had some brutal potholes this year. I ride my bike to the Metra station every morning and one of them almost sent me over the handlebars back in July. Reported it online, honestly forgot about it, and then came out last Monday to find the whole block repaved — not just patched. Really impressive. Whoever coordinates that deserves a high-five.

subject: Thank you for the new crosswalk on Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, the new painted crosswalk and flashing pedestrian signal at Bellwood Ave and 50th have been up for about three weeks and I already feel so much safer walking my granddaughter to school. Cars are actually stopping now. I know infrastructure projects take a long time to get funded and approved, so I imagine this has been in the works for a while. I just wanted to make sure someone at the top hears that it was worth it. Thank you very much.

subject: missed garbage made right
body_clean: mayor — hey so last week garbage didn't get picked up on my block (rice/granville area). called public works, woman who answered said she'd flag it. figured nothing would happen but literally the next morning a truck came back just for our block. that's pretty solid. thanks for making that work. not always the case in other towns.

subject: Incredibly fast response to icy intersection at Hirsch and 19th
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — I emailed your office Tuesday evening about black ice at Hirsch and 19th Ave after I nearly slipped carrying my baby. Someone called me back Wednesday morning and a crew with sand and salt was there by noon. I was honestly shocked — I expected a form letter. That's real responsiveness and I appreciate it more than I can say. Little moments like that are why people choose to stay in Bellwood.

subject: Incredibly fast response to icy intersection at Hirsch and 19th
body_clean: Ms. Nataraj, your note made my morning. Hirsch and 19th was already on Frank's watch list this winter because of drainage patterns there, so the crew had eyes on it. But hearing from you directly — and knowing your family is safe — is exactly the kind of feedback that keeps everyone motivated. Comments like yours go on the bulletin board in the Public Works garage. Thank you.

subject: Library renovation looks great
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo. I know you don't run the library directly but I figured you'd want to know it's getting compliments. The Bellwood Public Library renovation is really something — the new reading area is bright and welcoming, the kids' section is expanded, and the staff seem energized by the space. I've been coming here since I was nine years old and it's never looked this good. Whatever role the Village played in making that happen, I'm grateful.

subject: Drainage repair on Geneva Ave — what a difference
body_clean: Mayor, my basement has flooded every spring since 2019. We had the new catch basin installed on the 47 block of Geneva Ave last fall and this spring — the wettest March I can remember — not a drop. I want to cry, honestly. I know the drainage projects take years to fund and plan and I know we weren't the only block waiting. But I want you to know it changed everything for my family. Please keep investing in this infrastructure. It matters enormously.

subject: Drainage repair on Geneva Ave — what a difference
body_clean: Ms. Grabowski, Frank DiMeo here from Public Works. The Mayor forwarded your note and asked me to reach out directly. That Geneva Ave basin was a tricky install — older clay tile feeding into an undersized main — so it's genuinely satisfying to hear it performed through a wet March. We'll keep an eye on it through the rest of the season. If you see ponding on your side of the curb again before May, call us right away.

subject: Kudos on the Taste of Bellwood parking plan
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've attended the Taste of Bellwood for years and parking has always been a nightmare. This year was completely different — the signage was clear, the lot at the school was actually staffed, and traffic on St. Charles moved. I heard there was a new coordinator this year. Whoever planned the traffic and parking flow deserves a serious pat on the back. Best-run version of that event in recent memory. Thank you.

subject: Village Hall staff went above and beyond
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I came into Village Hall last Thursday to ask about a permit for a small fence and I was fully prepared to be shuffled around for an hour. Instead, a young woman at the front desk — I didn't catch her name, she had dark hair and a green sweater — walked me to the right office herself, explained what I'd need, and even made sure I had the correct form before I left. Start to finish: twelve minutes. That's remarkable. Please recognize whoever she is.

subject: Road crew showed up at 4am during the December storm
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo. I'm a nurse and I work nights at a hospital in Maywood. Getting out of my neighborhood at 4 a.m. during the December 17th storm, the plows were already running on Englewood Ave and Morris. I made it to work on time. I know people complain when things go wrong but I wanted you to hear from someone whose night went right because your crews were already out there before sunrise. Please thank them from me.

subject: Road crew showed up at 4am during the December storm
body_clean: Ms. Achebe, this message genuinely moved me. Our crews started pre-treating routes at 11 p.m. the night before that storm and didn't stand down until midday — long shifts in brutal conditions. I'm going to read your note at our next Public Works staff meeting. What you do as a nurse, and what they do overnight on those trucks, both matter. Thank you for writing, and thank you for the work you do.
